title: Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of 2025
short_title: Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law helps Alaska Native veterans who served in the military during the Vietnam War get free land. These veterans can receive up to 160 acres of federal land in Alaska. The program was supposed to end in December 2025, but this law extends it for five more years until December 2030. This gives Alaska Native Vietnam veterans and their families more time to apply for the land they earned through their military service.
subjects: Wilderness and natural areas, wildlife refuges, wild rivers, habitats

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Extension Act of two thousand and twenty-five.

sections.title: Section 2  Extension of the Alaska Native Vietnam Era Veterans Land Allotment Program
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the John D. Dingell, Jr. Conservation, Management, and Recreation Act. The change affects the Alaska Native Vietnam era veterans land allotment program. Where the old law said the program would last for a five-year period, this new law changes it to a ten-year period. This means Alaska Native veterans who served during the Vietnam era will have five more years to apply for land allotments under this program.
